title: Atropis conferta
intro: Atropis conferta, also known as Hare's Ear or Atropis, is a flowering plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate pain, reduce fever, and boost immune function. The bioactive constituents of Atropis conferta include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Atropis conferta include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Atropis conferta is classified as a species within the genus Atropis. The genus Atropis is a group of flowering plants in the family Apiaceae. Atropis conferta is further classified as a member of the order Apiales. The plant is native to China and is a type of flowering plant. Atropis conferta is closely related to other species within the Atropis genus.
